Student Council

Kadir Has University Student Assembly was established in October 2024 in order to enable students to take a more active role in academic, social and cultural life. This structure aims to strengthen communication between student communities and create an effective bridge between the university administration and students by providing a democratic platform where students from different faculties of the university are represented. The Student Assembly’s mission is to support students’ active participation in decision-making and to pioneer projects that will raise the academic and social standards of the university.

After its establishment, the Assembly aims to bring a breath of fresh air to university life by becoming a platform where students’ interests are defended, innovative ideas are discussed and implemented.

The Kadir Has University Student Council is composed of representatives from the English Preparatory School, the Core Curriculum Program, and the Faculties.

The Student Council Executive Board consists of a total of 7 representatives: one from each Faculty, one from the Preparatory School, and one from the Core Curriculum Program.

The Executive Board holds regular monthly meetings with the Rector, the Vice Rector for Education, and the Dean of Students. These meetings aim to ensure that the concerns and suggestions raised by our students are communicated to the university administration in a more systematic, effective, and inclusive manner, and that the discussed issues are followed up efficiently.
